title: oxyria digyna subsp. elatior
intro: Oxyria digyna subsp. elatior, also known as the high-altitude mountain sorrel, is a plant species that belongs to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae) and is native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas and the Tibetan Plateau. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Oxyria digyna subsp. elatior include reducing inflammation, improving antioxidant defenses, and supporting immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
